mitre_techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
mitre_techniques.description: Use application protocols for C2 communication

mitre_techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
mitre_techniques.description: Exfiltrate data over the C2 channel

explanation: The attacker likely used `wget` to exfiltrate data from the target system by sending it over a command-and-control (C2) channel established at IP address 1.1.1.1 on port 443. This activity maps to MITRE ATT&CK techniques T1071, Application Layer Protocol (Command and Control), and T1041, Exfiltration Over C2 Channel. To mitigate this threat, the next steps should include isolating the infected system from the network, conducting a thorough investigation of the compromised files, and implementing additional security measures to prevent future attacks.

mitre_techniques.name: Unix Shell
mitre_techniques.description: Abuse of Unix shell for command execution

mitre_techniques.name: Protocol Tunneling
mitre_techniques.description: Tunnel C2 through legitimate protocols

explanation: The attacker likely executed a malicious command using a Unix shell, which falls under the MITRE ATT&CK technique T1059.004 for abuse of Unix shell. This indicates that the attacker is attempting to establish a Command and Control (C2) channel through legitimate protocols, aligning with the T1572 technique for protocol tunneling. As next steps, one should immediately investigate the system logs to identify any further unusual activities, isolate the compromised host from the network to prevent potential data exfiltration, and consider conducting a full security assessment to ensure no other systems are affected.

mitre_techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
mitre_techniques.description: Use application protocols for C2 communication

mitre_techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
mitre_techniques.description: Exfiltrate data over the C2 channel

explanation: The attacker likely used the `wget` process to download or exfiltrate data from the target system, targeting the `/proc/cpuinfo` file for potential information gathering or data exfiltration purposes. This activity aligns with MITRE ATT&CK techniques T1071 (Application Layer Protocol - Command and Control) for establishing a command-and-control channel and T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel) for sending stolen data back to the attacker's infrastructure. The next steps should include identifying the source of `wget` execution, analyzing network traffic for signs of C2 communications, and implementing or enhancing security measures such as monitoring file access, updating antivirus definitions, and isolating the compromised system if necessary.

mitre_techniques.name: Unix Shell
mitre_techniques.description: Abuse of Unix shell for command execution

mitre_techniques.name: Unix Shell
mitre_techniques.description: Abuse of Unix shell for command execution

mitre_techniques.name: Protocol Tunneling
mitre_techniques.description: Tunnel C2 through legitimate protocols

explanation: Unix shell execution detected via bash (T1059.004: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell). While shell usage can be legitimate, the threat score of 68.29 indicates suspicious context. Action: review the command history, check for downloaded scripts, and verify the user's identity.

mitre_techniques.description: Tunnel C2 through legitimate protocols

mitre_techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
mitre_techniques.description: Exfiltrate data over the C2 channel

explanation: Process curl communicated with external IP 104.21.0.1 using application-layer protocols (T1071). This may indicate C2 communication or data exfiltration. Action: capture network traffic for analysis, block the destination IP, and review DNS logs for domain generation algorithm (DGA) patterns.
